description: Stable specification for persistent context generation. This spec is the source of truth — AI adapts to it, not vice versa. Ensures consistency across CLI tools and model versions.

generation.description: Use the CLI tool's native AI (Claude Code's Claude, Cursor's Claude, etc.) to generate context
generation.systemPrompt: You are an persistent context generator. Your job is to analyze project code and generate spec-compliant context files. Follow the generation-spec.json schema exactly. Output must be valid JSON and markdown.

agent_context.purpose: Ultra-compressed context block for the AI agent

sections.title: Critical Patterns (from SEED.md)
sections.example:
- All database queries use Prisma
- Auth routes protected with Clerk
- API responses wrapped in standardized envelope

sections.title: Hard Constraints
sections.example:
- Do not modify migration files
- All env vars prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_
- Database schema changes require PR approval

sections.title: Current Active Spec
sections.example:
See: SPECS/active/add-payments/
Read before implementing tasks

subsections.name: Data Layer (Database)
subsections.example:
- Schema defined in prisma/schema.prisma
- Migrations auto-generated
- All queries typed via Prisma SDK

subsections.name: Auth Layer
subsections.example:
- Use Clerk for user management
- Protect routes with middleware
- Sync user roles to database

subsections.name: API Layer
subsections.example:
- Use server actions for mutations
- REST endpoints for public APIs
- Validate input with Zod schemas

subsections.name: UI Layer
subsections.example:
- Build with shadcn components
- Style with Tailwind classes
- Use React hooks for state

subsections.name: Testing
subsections.example:
- Unit tests with Vitest
- E2E tests with Playwright
- >80% coverage required

subsections.name: Data Layer
subsections.example:
- Direct SQL queries (always use ORM)
- Manual migration files
- Dangling foreign keys

subsections.name: Auth
subsections.example:
- Never store passwords in code
- Don't hardcode API keys
- No direct role checks without audit logs

subsections.name: API
subsections.example:
- Unvalidated user input
- Responses without error wrapping
- No CORS protection

subsections.name: UI
subsections.example:
- Mixing Tailwind + CSS modules
- Inline styles in components
- Uncontrolled form inputs in React

subsections.name: Tech Choices
subsections.example: Why Next.js? Why Prisma? Why Clerk?

sections.title: Expected File Structure
sections.format: tree view with explanation of each directory

subsections.name: Setup
subsections.example:
npm install
npm run db:push
npm run dev

subsections.name: First Task
subsections.example:
Read AGENT_CONTEXT.md
Check active spec in SPECS/active/
Consult MEMORY/INDEX.md for context

memory_index.purpose: Hot notes synced from Obsidian vault

sections.title: Recent Notes (Last 7 days)
sections.format: list of note titles + brief excerpt

sections.title: Decisions & Rationale
sections.format: key decisions made, linked to ADRs

sections.title: Active Issues & Known Limitations
sections.format: issues the AI should know about

sections.title: Sync Information
sections.format: last sync time, vault path, tag filter
sections.example:
- Last sync: 2026-03-11 14:23 UTC
- Vault: /Users/me/Obsidian Vault
- Tags: #persistent, #workflow, #decision

codeAnalysis.description: Use CLI's AI to analyze actual project code

rules.description: Each file must stay within token limits
All required sections must be present
Output must follow exact format specified

forCliAI.description: Instructions that CLI's native AI should follow when generating context

systemPrompt.task: Analyze the project and generate three spec-compliant markdown files.
constraints: Follow the sections structure exactly as defined in generation-spec.json
Use the project's actual code patterns, not generic advice
Make output actionable and specific to THIS project

prompts.initial: Analyze the project structure and code to generate persistent context files.
prompts.codeAnalysis: Read these code files and identify the actual patterns being used.
prompts.patternExtraction: Based on the code, what patterns MUST new code follow?
prompts.constraintIdentification: What are the hard constraints that the AI must never violate?
prompts.antiPatternExtraction: What mistakes or patterns should be forbidden?

openspec.description: openspec.dev integration — the spec lifecycle engine
openspec.fallback: local file-based engine when CLI unavailable

lifecycle.init: Initialize SPECS/ folder structure with SEED.md
lifecycle.propose: Create a new feature spec under SPECS/active/<slug>/
lifecycle.validate: Validate spec against SEED constraints + generation-spec
lifecycle.archive: Move completed spec → SPECS/archived/, extract patterns, evolve SEED

obsidianBridge.description: Obsidian notes tagged #spec or #decision feed into spec proposals as context

skills.description: skills.sh integration — dynamic skill discovery and evolution
skills.fallback: builtin skills from config/builtin-skills/ when CLI unavailable

lifecycle.search: Search skills.sh registry for relevant skills
lifecycle.install: Install skills with version tracking in .skills/.manifest.json
lifecycle.update: Update installed skills to latest versions
lifecycle.create: Create skills from project patterns + Obsidian notes
lifecycle.evolve: Evolve existing skills with new patterns from code + specs

obsidianBridge.description: Obsidian notes tagged #pattern, #skill, #best-practice feed into skill creation/evolution

obsidian.description: Obsidian vault as bidirectional context layer binding specs + skills
obsidian.pullBehavior: Extract tagged notes from vault → route to openspec / skills / memory
obsidian.pushBehavior: Write-back: archived specs → vault, evolved skills → vault, SEED updates → vault

writebackFormats.spec: Markdown with YAML frontmatter (status, slug, archived date)
writebackFormats.skill: Markdown with YAML frontmatter (id, version, source)
writebackFormats.seed: Markdown snapshot of SEED.md with sync timestamp

stability.description: How this spec ensures stability across AI model & CLI changes
benefits: Spec is source of truth, not AI behavior
Switching Claude to GPT-4? Spec still works
Switching Claude Code to Cursor? Spec still works
Old AI model versions? New model versions? Spec handles both
Project can review & approve generated specs manually
Three-tool trinity (OpenSpec + Skills.sh + Obsidian) creates self-reinforcing context
Obsidian notes survive tool/AI changes as human-readable markdown
stability.versionStrategy: Semantic versioning. Major = breaking schema changes. Minor = new sections. Patch = clarifications.
stability.upgradeProcess: Regenerate context files with new spec version, review, approve, commit.
